title: VerdantPulse Systems: Soil Health Monitor
description: Welcome to the VerdantPulse Systems internal portal. As a newly contracted security auditor, your goal is to investigate the Field Management dashboard. The developers have implemented a basic WAF to prevent common attacks, but rumors suggest that the 'Custom Sorting' feature in the field settings might be processing data unsafely. Retrieve the master system key (flag) stored within the configuration tables.
solution: 1. Log in using the provided guest credentials (guest / guest2024). 2. Navigate to the 'Field Dashboard' and click 'Settings' for any field (e.g., North Plot). 3. Identify that the 'Sort Preference' can be updated. This value is stored in the database and later used in an ORDER BY clause on the sensors page. 4. The update endpoint has a WAF that blocks 'SELECT', 'UNION', 'WHERE', and spaces. 5. Bypass the WAF using SQLite comments /**/ for spaces and case-insensitive/inline-commented keywords like SEL/**/ECT. 6. Inject a CASE statement into the sort_pref field: `(CASE/**/WHEN/**/(SEL/**/ECT/**/cat_val/**/FROM/**/site_config/**/WH/**/ERE/**/cat_key='sys.auth.entropy_seed')/**/LIKE/**/'L%')/**/THEN/**/ph_level/**/ELSE/**/nitrogen/**/END`. 7. Visit the sensor logs page for that field. If the logs are sorted by PH level, the condition was true. If they are sorted by Nitrogen, it was false. 8. Script the boolean extraction to recover the full flag character by character.

narrative_context.scenario:
VerdantPulse Systems is a leader in precision agriculture, providing IoT-enabled soil monitoring and automated irrigation systems to industrial-scale farms across the Midwest. Their centralized management platform allows farm managers to monitor nitrogen levels, soil moisture, and pH across thousands of acres in real-time. Recently, VerdantPulse acquired a smaller boutique sensor manufacturer and quickly integrated their legacy data ingestion systems into the main corporate infrastructure to meet a quarterly rollout deadline.

The integration was overseen by a skeleton crew from the DevOps team while the primary security engineers were focused on a high-priority SOC2 audit. While the interface looks modern and polished, the backend connectors for the legacy 'Field-Link' data search tool were migrated with minimal refactoring. The company is now preparing for a major partnership with a national grain cooperative and needs to ensure that the data silos between different farm holdings are properly isolated.
narrative_context.app_purpose: Provides farm administrators with the ability to query historical soil health data and schedule automated nutrient dispersal across specific plots of land.
narrative_context.data_theme: Agricultural sensor logs, field coordinates, soil chemistry metrics, and farm manager credentials.

flavor_details.recent_event: A recent internal audit flagged the 'Field-Link' legacy module as a potential risk due to its age and the speed of its integration into the main portal.

calibration_notes: Source analysis: This is a second-order blind SQL injection in an ORDER BY clause. Exploitation requires bypassing a WAF that forbids spaces and common keywords by using SQL comments (/**/) and crafting a boolean-based oracle that changes the returned row order (e.g., sorting by ph_level vs nitrogen) to extract the flag character-by-character. (target was 1.5)
